summary: 0.6.56 stops adjusting analog movie scrub formulas and routes non-trigger movie joystick input through the exact existing 15-second skip transport, because the operator wants the joystick to match the skip button behavior directly while keeping image stepping intact.
operatorSignals: 0.6.54 still made movie joystick movement feel tiny, around one second.
0.6.55 overshot badly and moved around two minutes on long videos.
The operator explicitly asked for the same behavior as the existing skip-forward control and did not want another new movie scrub experiment.
actionsTaken: Removed the analog movie scrub-rate experiment from the non-trigger movie joystick branch.
Reused the exact existing 15-second skip transport only in the non-trigger movie joystick branch.
Left image stepping and trigger-modified video behavior alone.
nonGoals: No slideshow work in this slice.
No right-stick volume, gaze, or scene-builder changes in this slice.
No change to image stepping behavior in this slice.
